title: Coelogyne unguiculata
taxonomy: Coelogyne unguiculata is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family, commonly known as the orchid family, is the largest family of flowering plants. The genus Coelogyne is a type of orchid known for its single, long-lasting flowers and is native to tropical regions of Asia. Coelogyne unguiculata is specifically found in the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. It is a terrestrial orchid that grows in humid, shaded areas.

intro: Coelogyne unguiculata, also known as the beaked Coelogyne or the beaked orchid, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne unguiculata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelogyne unguiculata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of extracts or tinctures.
